meaning_en: To formally put an end to a law, system, or practice
example: Many countries have abolished the death penalty.
mnemonic: ABOLISH — A + POLISH; polish away the old rule until it's gone.

meaning_en: Cruel or violent treatment; to misuse something
example: Abuse of power by leaders must be challenged.
mnemonic: ABUSE — A + BUSE; a BRUISE from ABUSE.

meaning_en: The fact of being responsible for your actions
example: Accountability in government builds public trust.
mnemonic: ACCOUNTABILITY — ACCOUNT + ABILITY; the ABILITY to ACCOUNT for actions.

meaning_en: To gather or collect over time
example: She accumulated years of experience before starting her own firm.
mnemonic: ACCUMULATE — ACCU + MULATE; mules carry loads; accumulate what you carry.

meaning_en: A person who campaigns strongly for political or social change
example: Environmental activists protested outside the factory.
mnemonic: ACTIVIST — ACTIVE + IST; someone who is ACTIVELY fighting for a cause.

meaning_en: The process of changing to suit new conditions
example: Adaptation to climate change is now an urgent priority.
mnemonic: ADAPTATION — ADAPT + ATION; the result of adapting.

meaning_en: To publicly recommend or support; a person who does this
example: She advocates for equal rights in the workplace.
mnemonic: ADVOCATE — AD + VOICE + ATE; give your VOICE loudly for a cause.

meaning_en: The consequences or effects of an unpleasant event
example: The aftermath of the earthquake lasted for years.
mnemonic: AFTERMATH — AFTER + MATH; the MATH (count) of damage AFTER disaster.

meaning_en: A union or partnership formed for mutual benefit
example: NATO is a military alliance of Western nations.
mnemonic: ALLIANCE — ALL + IANCE; ALL joined = alliance.

meaning_en: A claim that someone has done something wrong, without proof yet
example: The politician denied all allegations of corruption.
mnemonic: ALLEGATION — ALLEGE + ATION; what is alleged becomes an allegation.

meaning_en: A diplomat representing their country in another country
example: The Indian ambassador met with French officials.
mnemonic: AMBASSADOR — AMBASS + ADOR; a grand ADORned representative.

meaning_en: A change made to a law or document
example: The First Amendment protects freedom of speech in the US.
mnemonic: AMENDMENT — AMEND + MENT; the result of amending (fixing) a law.

meaning_en: A violent attack on someone; to attack someone violently
example: He was charged with assault after the fight.
mnemonic: ASSAULT — A + SALT; throwing SALT in someone's wound = assault.

meaning_en: A group of people meeting together; the act of gathering
example: The UN General Assembly meets every September.
mnemonic: ASSEMBLY — ASSEMBLE + Y; why ASSEMBLE? To form an assembly.

meaning_en: Protection given by a government to someone who has fled their country
example: She was granted asylum after fleeing political persecution.
mnemonic: ASYLUM — A + SYLUM; a SAFE LOOM (shelter) for the displaced.

meaning_en: The right to self-govern; independence
example: The region demanded greater political autonomy.
mnemonic: AUTONOMY — AUTO + NOMY; AUTO (self) + NOMY (rule) = self-rule.

meaning_en: A process of voting, usually done in secret
example: The result was decided by a secret ballot.
mnemonic: BALLOT — BALL + OT; a BALL rolled into the box = ballot.

meaning_en: An obstacle that prevents movement or progress
example: Language barriers can hinder communication.
mnemonic: BARRIER — BAR + RIER; a BAR blocks the way.

meaning_en: An important discovery or development
example: Scientists made a breakthrough in cancer research.
mnemonic: BREAKTHROUGH — BREAK + THROUGH; BREAK THROUGH the wall of limitation.

meaning_en: A heavy load; a responsibility that causes hardship
example: The cost of healthcare is a burden for many families.
mnemonic: BURDEN — BURN + DEN; a burning DEN of responsibility = burden.

meaning_en: An organised effort to achieve a social or political goal
example: The charity ran a campaign to raise awareness.
mnemonic: CAMPAIGN — CAMP + AIGN; a military CAMP fighting for a cause.

meaning_en: A person killed or injured in a war or accident
example: The conflict resulted in heavy casualties on both sides.
mnemonic: CASUALTY — CASUAL + TY; something CASUALLY dangerous becomes a casualty.

meaning_en: Complete disorder and confusion
example: The sudden power cut caused chaos in the office.
mnemonic: CHAOS — a CAT in a HOSe = CHAOS; unpredictable disorder.

meaning_en: Persisting for a long time; always present
example: He suffers from chronic back pain.
mnemonic: CHRONIC — CHRONO (time) + IC; lasting through time = chronic.

meaning_en: A person not in the armed forces or police
example: The army was accused of targeting civilians.
mnemonic: CIVILIAN — CIVIL + IAN; someone living a CIVIL (non-military) life.

meaning_en: A temporary alliance of political parties or groups
example: A coalition government was formed after the election.
mnemonic: COALITION — COAL + ITION; coal pieces joining = forming a coalition.

meaning_en: To work jointly with others on a project
example: Scientists from ten countries collaborated on the study.
mnemonic: COLLABORATE — CO + LABOUR + ATE; labour together = collaborate.

meaning_en: Sympathetic concern for the suffering of others
example: A good doctor treats patients with skill and compassion.
mnemonic: COMPASSION — COM + PASSION; shared PASSION for others' pain = compassion.

meaning_en: Evoking interest and attention; powerfully convincing
example: She made a compelling argument for change.
mnemonic: COMPELLING — COMPEL + LING; something that COMPELS you to listen.

meaning_en: An agreement reached by each side giving something up
example: Both parties reached a compromise after long negotiations.
mnemonic: COMPROMISE — COMPRO + MISE; put MISE en place — arrange the middle ground.

meaning_en: An inner sense of what is right or wrong
example: Her conscience would not let her stay silent.
mnemonic: CONSCIENCE — CON + SCIENCE; the inner SCIENCE of knowing right from wrong.

meaning_en: A general agreement among a group
example: There is no consensus on how to solve the climate crisis.
mnemonic: CONSENSUS — CON + SENSUS; sensing together = consensus.

meaning_en: The fundamental laws and principles governing a state
example: India's Constitution guarantees equality for all citizens.
mnemonic: CONSTITUTION — CONSTITUTE + ION; what constitutes the law of the land.

meaning_en: Dishonest or fraudulent conduct by those in power
example: Corruption undermines democracy and justice.
mnemonic: CORRUPTION — CORRUPT + ION; a corroded ION (element) in society.

meaning_en: The quality of being trusted and believed
example: The scandal damaged the company's credibility.
mnemonic: CREDIBILITY — CREDIT + ABILITY; the ABILITY to earn CREDIT = credibility.

meaning_en: A time of intense difficulty requiring urgent decisions
example: The government acted swiftly during the economic crisis.
mnemonic: CRISIS — CRY + SIS; everyone CRIES when a crisis hits.

meaning_en: Protective care; legal guardianship; being held by police
example: The suspect was taken into police custody.
mnemonic: CUSTODY — CUST + ODY; the BODY (ody) in CUSTOM (cust) control = custody.

meaning_en: A formal discussion of opposing viewpoints
example: The presidential debate was watched by millions.
mnemonic: DEBATE — DE + BATE; bait both sides into a DEBATE.

meaning_en: A formal announcement or statement
example: The Declaration of Independence was signed in 1776.
mnemonic: DECLARATION — DECLARE + ATION; saying it loud and clear = declaration.

meaning_en: Strong commitment to something over time
example: Her dedication to the sport earned her a gold medal.
mnemonic: DEDICATION — DEDICATE + ION; the state of being dedicated.

meaning_en: A statement that something is not true; a refusal to acknowledge
example: He issued a firm denial of all charges.
mnemonic: DENIAL — DE + NILE; like the NILE river, denial just FLOWS past the truth.

meaning_en: A system of government where citizens vote for leaders
example: India is the world's largest democracy.
mnemonic: DEMOCRACY — DEMO (people) + CRACY (rule); ruled by the people.

meaning_en: The act of moving troops or resources into action
example: The government approved the deployment of troops.
mnemonic: DEPLOYMENT — DEPLOY + MENT; the act of deploying forces.

meaning_en: The state of being held in custody
example: He was held in detention without charge for 48 hours.
mnemonic: DETENTION — DETAIN + TION; the result of being detained.

meaning_en: The quality of being worthy of honour and respect
example: Every person deserves to be treated with dignity.
mnemonic: DIGNITY — DIG + NITY; DIG deep into your IDENTITY = dignity.

meaning_en: A situation requiring a choice between difficult options
example: She faced a dilemma: leave or stay and fight.
mnemonic: DILEMMA — DI (two) + LEMMA (problem); two equally tough problems.

meaning_en: A government official who manages relations with other countries
example: The diplomat worked to resolve the border dispute.
mnemonic: DIPLOMAT — DIPLO (double) + MAT; managing two sides on a MAT of negotiation.

meaning_en: Unjust treatment of different groups of people
example: Discrimination based on race or gender is illegal.
mnemonic: DISCRIMINATION — DISCRIM + INATION; criminalizing a nation's differences.

meaning_en: A disturbance that interrupts an event or process
example: The strike caused major disruption to transport.
mnemonic: DISRUPTION — DISRUPT + ION; the force of disrupting things.

meaning_en: The range of different types of people or things
example: The company values diversity and inclusion.
mnemonic: DIVERSITY — DIVERSE + ITY; the state of being diverse.

meaning_en: Most powerful or influential
example: English is the dominant language in global business.
mnemonic: DOMINANT — DOMAIN + ANT; ruling the domain = dominant.

meaning_en: Achieving maximum productivity with minimum wasted effort
example: Energy efficiency reduces both cost and pollution.
mnemonic: EFFICIENCY — EFFECT + ICIENCY; being most EFFECTIVE = efficiency.

meaning_en: A group regarded as superior in ability or status
example: Only the elite could afford to attend the event.
mnemonic: ELITE — E + LITE; the LITE (light) few at the top = elite.

meaning_en: To give someone the authority or strength to do something
example: Education empowers women in developing nations.
mnemonic: EMPOWER — EM + POWER; give POWER to others = empower.

meaning_en: The act of compelling obedience to a law or rule
example: Strict enforcement of traffic rules saves lives.
mnemonic: ENFORCEMENT — ENFORCE + MENT; the act of enforcing rules.

meaning_en: A widespread occurrence of an infectious disease
example: The cholera epidemic spread rapidly in the flood zones.
mnemonic: EPIDEMIC — EPI (on) + DEMIC (people); a disease spreading ON the people.

meaning_en: The state of being equal, especially in rights and opportunities
example: Gender equality remains a major global challenge.
mnemonic: EQUALITY — EQUAL + ITY; the state of being EQUAL.

meaning_en: The founding of an institution; the established social order
example: The establishment of democracy took decades.
mnemonic: ESTABLISHMENT — ESTABLISH + MENT; the result of establishing.

meaning_en: The act of treating someone unfairly for personal gain
example: Child labour is a form of exploitation.
mnemonic: EXPLOITATION — EXPLOIT + ATION; exploiting someone = exploitation.

meaning_en: To make something easier or help it happen
example: Technology facilitates communication across borders.
mnemonic: FACILITATE — FACIL (easy) + ITATE; make it easy = facilitate.

meaning_en: The development of events beyond one's control
example: She accepted her fate with courage.
mnemonic: FATE — LATE; your FATE decides when it's too LATE to change.

meaning_en: Violent and aggressive; very strong or intense
example: There was fierce competition for the top position.
mnemonic: FIERCE — FIRE + CE; a FIRE is fierce — intense and uncontrollable.

meaning_en: To run away from danger or an unpleasant situation
example: Thousands fled the country during the war.
mnemonic: FLEE — FLEA; a FLEA flees from the scratch.

meaning_en: To grow or develop in a healthy or vigorous way
example: Small businesses flourish in a supportive economy.
mnemonic: FLOURISH — FLOUR + ISH; like FLOUR rising in bread, things FLOURISH.

meaning_en: A basic structure underlying a system or concept
example: A legal framework is needed to regulate AI.
mnemonic: FRAMEWORK — FRAME + WORK; a FRAME within which things WORK.

meaning_en: Of central importance; forming the essential basis
example: Freedom of speech is a fundamental right.
mnemonic: FUNDAMENTAL — FUND + AMENTAL; the FUND (base) is fundamental.

meaning_en: The deliberate killing of a large group of people based on ethnicity
example: The Holocaust is one of history's worst acts of genocide.
mnemonic: GENOCIDE — GENO (race) + CIDE (kill); killing of a race = genocide.

meaning_en: The way an organisation or country is managed
example: Good governance requires transparency and accountability.
mnemonic: GOVERNANCE — GOVERN + ANCE; the practice of governing.

meaning_en: Aggressive pressure or intimidation of someone
example: Workplace harassment must be reported immediately.
mnemonic: HARASSMENT — HARASS + MENT; the act of harassing someone.

meaning_en: Cultural traditions passed down from previous generations
example: India's heritage is reflected in its art, music, and architecture.
mnemonic: HERITAGE — HEIR + TAGE; what the HEIR receives = heritage.

meaning_en: A system in which people are ranked above one another
example: The corporate hierarchy determines who makes decisions.
mnemonic: HIERARCHY — HIGH + ARCHY; HIGH ruling ARCH = hierarchy.

meaning_en: Unfriendly or aggressive behaviour
example: The crowd reacted with hostility to the announcement.
mnemonic: HOSTILITY — HOST + ILITY; a bad HOST creates hostility.

meaning_en: Concerned with improving the welfare of people
example: Aid workers provided humanitarian assistance to refugees.
mnemonic: HUMANITARIAN — HUMAN + ITARIAN; serving HUMAN needs = humanitarian.

meaning_en: A proposed explanation to be tested by research
example: Scientists tested the hypothesis through experiments.
mnemonic: HYPOTHESIS — HYPO (under) + THESIS; an under-the-surface theory.

meaning_en: A system of ideas and ideals forming the basis of a political theory
example: The party's ideology focuses on social equality.
mnemonic: IDEOLOGY — IDEA + OLOGY; the study (-ology) of IDEAS.

meaning_en: About to happen very soon
example: A storm was imminent — we had to take cover.
mnemonic: IMMINENT — IM + MINENT; looming overhead (eminent) = imminent.

meaning_en: The basic physical systems of a country or organisation
example: Investment in infrastructure boosts economic growth.
mnemonic: INFRASTRUCTURE — INFRA (below) + STRUCTURE; the foundational structure BELOW everything.

meaning_en: Lack of fairness or justice
example: She dedicated her life to fighting injustice.
mnemonic: INJUSTICE — IN + JUSTICE; the OPPOSITE of justice.

meaning_en: The mixing or combining of groups into one system
example: The integration of immigrants into society takes time.
mnemonic: INTEGRATION — INTEGER (whole) + RATION; making a whole = integration.

meaning_en: The quality of being honest and having strong moral principles
example: A leader must act with integrity at all times.
mnemonic: INTEGRITY — INTEGER (whole); being wholehearted and honest = integrity.

meaning_en: Action taken to change or improve a situation
example: Military intervention in the conflict was controversial.
mnemonic: INTERVENTION — INTER (between) + VENTION; coming BETWEEN two parties.

meaning_en: A systematic inquiry to discover facts
example: The police launched an investigation into the fraud.
mnemonic: INVESTIGATION — INVEST + IGATION; investing effort into finding the truth.

meaning_en: Fair treatment and the upholding of law
example: Every citizen deserves access to justice.
mnemonic: JUSTICE — JUST + ICE; cold hard JUST ICE = justice.

meaning_en: An important event or turning point; a notable building
example: The 1994 elections were a landmark moment in South Africa.
mnemonic: LANDMARK — LAND + MARK; a MARK on the LAND worth noting.

meaning_en: A legal case brought before a court
example: She filed a lawsuit against her former employer.
mnemonic: LAWSUIT — LAW + SUIT; wearing a SUIT in a court of LAW.

meaning_en: Something handed down by a predecessor; lasting impact
example: Gandhi's legacy continues to inspire millions.
mnemonic: LEGACY — LEG + ACY; your LEGS walk forward, but your legacy stays behind.

meaning_en: Laws made by a governing body
example: New legislation was passed to protect workers' rights.
mnemonic: LEGISLATION — LEGISLATE + ION; the act of legislating = legislation.

meaning_en: Conforming to the law; reasonable and justifiable
example: The protesters had legitimate concerns about the policy.
mnemonic: LEGITIMATE — LEGIT + IMATE; LEGIT = real and lawful = legitimate.

meaning_en: The state of being free from oppression or restriction
example: The Statue of Liberty is a symbol of freedom.
mnemonic: LIBERTY — LIBRE (free in Spanish) + TY; state of being FREE.

meaning_en: The ideas and opinions accepted by most people
example: The movement eventually entered the mainstream.
mnemonic: MAINSTREAM — MAIN + STREAM; the MAIN flowing STREAM of society.

meaning_en: Required by law or rule; compulsory
example: Wearing helmets is mandatory for motorcyclists.
mnemonic: MANDATORY — MANDATE + ORY; relating to a MANDATE (official order).

meaning_en: Controlling or influencing someone in a clever or dishonest way
example: The campaign used data manipulation to influence voters.
mnemonic: MANIPULATION — MANIPULATE + ION; twisting hands (mani = hand) to control.

meaning_en: The brutal killing of a large number of people
example: The massacre shocked the international community.
mnemonic: MASSACRE — MASS + ACRE; mass destruction of ACRES of lives.

meaning_en: The act of mediating between parties in a dispute
example: Mediation helped the two companies reach an agreement.
mnemonic: MEDIATION — MEDIATE + ION; coming in the MIDDLE to help both sides.

meaning_en: The movement of people from one place to another
example: Economic migration is increasing across the world.
mnemonic: MIGRATION — MIGRATE + ION; the act of migrating.

meaning_en: The force or speed of movement; growing strength
example: The campaign gained momentum after the speech.
mnemonic: MOMENTUM — MOMENT + UM; a MOMENT of energy building up = momentum.

meaning_en: Principles about what is right and wrong behaviour
example: Questions of morality are central to politics.
mnemonic: MORALITY — MORAL + ITY; the state of having morals.

meaning_en: Famous for something bad or negative
example: The area is notorious for high crime rates.
mnemonic: NOTORIOUS — NOT + ORIOUS; NOT glorious = notorious.

meaning_en: A sudden occurrence of something unwanted, like disease or war
example: An outbreak of typhoid affected thousands.
mnemonic: OUTBREAK — OUT + BREAK; a problem BREAKS OUT of control.

meaning_en: Very great in amount; impossible to resist
example: The response to the disaster was overwhelming.
mnemonic: OVERWHELMING — OVER + WHELM; a WAVE that goes OVER you = overwhelming.

meaning_en: A person who vigorously supports their country
example: He was celebrated as a patriot who died for his country.
mnemonic: PATRIOT — PATRI (father) + OT; defending the FATHERLAND = patriot.

meaning_en: A formal request signed by many people
example: Over 100,000 people signed the petition.
mnemonic: PETITION — PET + ITION; like a PET begging for something = petition.

meaning_en: A person who develops or introduces new ideas
example: Marie Curie was a pioneer in the field of radioactivity.
mnemonic: PIONEER — PIE + ONE + ER; the first one to bake the PIE = pioneer.

meaning_en: A solemn promise or commitment
example: The prime minister pledged to reduce poverty.
mnemonic: PLEDGE — LEDGE; stand on the LEDGE and make a PLEDGE.

meaning_en: An unfair opinion formed without enough knowledge
example: Prejudice against minorities must be challenged.
mnemonic: PREJUDICE — PRE + JUDGE; judging BEFORE knowing = prejudice.

meaning_en: Widespread respect and admiration earned by success
example: Winning the Nobel Prize brought her international prestige.
mnemonic: PRESTIGE — PRES (press) + TIGE; the PRESS (media) covering your PRESTIGE.

meaning_en: Information used to promote a particular viewpoint, often biased
example: The government used propaganda to justify the war.
mnemonic: PROPAGANDA — PROPAG + ANDA; spreading (propagating) a AGENDA.

meaning_en: The action of taking legal proceedings against someone
example: The prosecution presented strong evidence in court.
mnemonic: PROSECUTION — PROSECUTE + ION; the act of prosecuting in court.

meaning_en: A public demonstration of objection to something
example: Thousands joined the protest against police brutality.
mnemonic: PROTEST — PRO + TEST; put your beliefs to the TEST publicly = protest.

meaning_en: A principal administrative division of a country
example: The northern province was hit hardest by the floods.
mnemonic: PROVINCE — PROVE + INCE; a region that proves its independence.

meaning_en: Prejudice and discrimination based on race
example: Racism has no place in a civilised society.
mnemonic: RACISM — RACE + ISM; an ideology (ISM) based on RACE.

meaning_en: Drastic; relating to fundamental change
example: Radical reform is needed to fix the healthcare system.
mnemonic: RADICAL — RADIC (root) + AL; going to the ROOT = radical.

meaning_en: A mass meeting; to come together for a common cause
example: The opposition held a massive rally in the capital.
mnemonic: RALLY — REALLY together; a RALLY gets REAL support from the masses.

meaning_en: An organised resistance or uprising against authority
example: The rebellion against the dictator began in the south.
mnemonic: REBELLION — REBEL + LION; a REBEL with the heart of a LION = rebellion.

meaning_en: A public vote on a single political question
example: A referendum was held on whether to leave the EU.
mnemonic: REFERENDUM — REFER + ENDUM; REFER the decision ENDUM (to the end) = people decide.

meaning_en: A person forced to leave their country due to persecution or disaster
example: Millions of refugees fled the civil war.
mnemonic: REFUGEE — REFUGE + E; a person seeking REFUGE = refugee.

meaning_en: A system of government, especially an authoritarian one
example: The regime cracked down on political opponents.
mnemonic: REGIME — REGI (king) + ME; a king-like ME ruling = regime.

meaning_en: Unwilling and hesitant; not eager
example: He was reluctant to admit that he was wrong.
mnemonic: RELUCTANT — RE + LUCTANT; like a RELUCTANT wrestler who won't fight.

meaning_en: Known and admired by many people
example: She is renowned for her work in neuroscience.
mnemonic: RENOWNED — RE + KNOWN + ED; widely KNOWN and RE-known = renowned.

meaning_en: Opposition to something; refusal to accept or comply
example: The resistance movement fought for independence.
mnemonic: RESISTANCE — RESIST + ANCE; the act of resisting.
